title: Beef & Mushroom Orzo
summary: Beefy, mushroomy, cheesy orzo magic. A creamy, one-pan, weeknight comfort that delivers way more than it should.

method.description: Thinly slice the mushrooms and garlic. Pick the parsley leaves and thinly slice the stems.
Place a frying pan with high sides over a medium-high heat (no oil). Once hot, add the mushrooms, season with salt and cook, stirring, until browned, 10-15 mins. Tip onto a plate.
Pour the olive oil into the empty pan. Once hot, add the beef mince, season with salt and pepper, and cook for 5–8 mins, until browned all over.
Add the orzo, parsley stems, garlic and plenty of black pepper to the pan. Cook for a couple of minutes until the garlic is fragrant.
Pour in the beef stock and water. Stir to combine, then cook the orzo until al dente, following pack instructions. Add a splash of water if it starts to look dry.
Once the orzo is cooked, stir through the mushrooms and three-quarters of the Parmesan. Taste and season with more salt and pepper, if needed.
Share between bowls and top with the remaining Parmesan, a crack of black pepper and parsley leaves.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
